brief: Global tensions continue to escalate, with Amnesty International calling for investigations into Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon as potential war crimes, while the International Monetary Fund notes that the global economy remains resilient despite these conflicts. In India, economic growth forecasts have been revised downward due to higher oil prices, with the Asian Development Bank cutting its growth forecast for FY27 to 6.6%. Meanwhile, the Indian government is emphasizing the importance of dialogue and diplomacy in resolving international conflicts, with Prime Minister Narendra Modi advocating for diplomatic engagement as the preferred approach to managing global tensions.

Geopolitics.title: Amnesty urges investigating Israeli attacks on Lebanon as ‘war crimes’
Geopolitics.summary: Amnesty International has called for investigations into Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon, considering them potential war crimes against civilians. This development highlights the ongoing tensions in the region and the need for accountability. The situation underscores the complexities of conflict in the Middle East.

Geopolitics.title: Global economy resilient despite war: IMF
Geopolitics.summary: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has stated that the global economy remains resilient despite the economic shocks caused by the conflict in the Middle East. This assessment indicates the economy's ability to withstand geopolitical tensions. The IMF's view is significant for understanding the interplay between global economics and geopolitics.

Geopolitics.title: Why Ukraine's air shield works against drones but struggles against ballistic missiles
Geopolitics.summary: Ukraine's air defense system has shown effectiveness against drones but faces challenges in countering ballistic missiles. This disparity highlights the complexities of modern warfare and the evolving nature of threats. The situation in Ukraine provides insights into the strategic and technological aspects of contemporary conflicts.

Geopolitics.title: Malaysia's Strategic Energy Moves: Navigating International Trade with Russia
Geopolitics.summary: Malaysia is exploring the possibility of importing oil from Russia, navigating international trade norms amidst Western sanctions. This move reflects Malaysia's efforts to diversify its energy sources and maintain trade relations with Russia. The development is noteworthy for understanding the geopolitical dynamics of energy trade.

Geopolitics.title: 'Only dialogue and diplomacy can resolve West Asia conflict': PM Modi
Geopolitics.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has emphasized the importance of dialogue and diplomacy in resolving conflicts in West Asia. He reiterated India's commitment to promoting peace, stability, and a rules-based international order. This stance reflects India's approach to geopolitical issues and its role in regional and global affairs.

Geopolitics.title: PM Modi Advocates Diplomacy Amid Rising Global Tensions
Geopolitics.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has advocated for diplomatic engagement as the preferred approach to resolving international conflicts, especially amid rising tensions in West Asia. He promoted economic and security cooperation during his visit to Australia. Modi's emphasis on diplomacy highlights India's perspective on managing global tensions and conflicts.

Economy.title: ADB cuts India FY27 growth forecast to 6.6% on higher oil prices
Economy.summary: The Asian Development Bank has reduced India's growth forecast for FY27 to 6.6% due to higher crude oil prices and transportation costs. This is expected to impact consumer sentiment and demand, with inflation forecast raised to 5.2%. The bank expects global conditions to improve in FY28, with a retained growth forecast of 7.3%.

Economy.title: Gujarat unveils data centre policy to attract AI investments; State offers capital, power and tax incentives
Economy.summary: Gujarat has introduced a new data centre policy to attract significant AI investments, aiming to establish itself as India's premier data centre hub. The state is offering comprehensive fiscal and non-fiscal incentives, including subsidies on capital, interest, and power tariffs. This move is expected to boost competitiveness and attract businesses to the state.

Economy.title: India wants to lead, not just host, GCCs: Nirmala Sitharaman
Economy.summary: India aims to lead the world in global capability centres and the knowledge economy, shifting its focus from scale to enabling innovation and shaping future technologies. The country currently hosts over half of the world's GCCs, and the government is creating an enabling policy ecosystem to support future growth. Enterprises are prioritizing innovation and long-term competitiveness over cost reduction.

Economy.title: RBI launches July round of rural, urban consumer confidence and inflation expectation surveys
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has launched its July 2026 round of household surveys to gather public feedback on economic conditions, inflation, and employment. The surveys will provide inputs for monetary policy, covering urban and rural consumer confidence, as well as inflation expectations. The findings will help the RBI make informed decisions on economic policy.

Economy.title: Bank credit growth to ease as corporates tap bond markets, overseas funding: CareEdge
Economy.summary: Bank credit growth is expected to moderate in the upcoming fiscal year as large corporations increasingly use domestic bond markets and overseas borrowings for funding. The Reserve Bank of India's policy measures have made external funding more attractive for businesses, reducing reliance on traditional bank loans. Overall credit demand remains healthy, but banks will finance a smaller share of corporate financing needs.

Business.title: Bank credit growth to ease as corporates tap bond markets, overseas funding: CareEdge
Business.summary: Bank credit growth is expected to slow down as large corporations increasingly use domestic bond markets and overseas borrowings for funding, reducing their reliance on traditional bank loans. This shift is driven by the Reserve Bank of India's policy measures that make external funding more attractive. Overall credit demand remains healthy, but banks will finance a smaller share.

Business.title: Neuberg readies for IPO in FY28, to seek up to Rs 4,000 cr
Business.summary: Neuberg Diagnostics plans to launch an initial public offering in fiscal year 2028, aiming to raise approximately ₹3,500-4,000 crore. The company also plans a pre-IPO funding round to secure Rs 5000 crore for acquisitions, targeting pan-India growth and new diagnostic technologies. This expansion strategy aims to make Neuberg the second-largest player in the diagnostics market.

Business.title: Corporate Paper issuances touch 5-yr high at Rs 2.53 lakh crore in June
Business.summary: Indian companies raised a significant amount of funds via commercial papers in June, reaching a near five-year high of Rs 2.53 lakh crore. This surge is driven by attractive pricing, seasonal needs, and debt refinancing, with companies shifting from costlier bank loans to cheaper short-term market funding. The Reserve Bank of India's liquidity infusions also contributed to favorable conditions.

Business.title: India's banks are handing out more loans, but deposits aren't keeping up
Business.summary: Indian banks have seen a surge in lending, outpacing deposit growth and widening the funding gap, with the loan-to-deposit ratio reaching a decade-high. This trend is driven by robust demand from corporate and retail sectors, raising concerns about funding sustainability. Banks are adopting varied strategies to manage this gap amidst intense competition.

Business.title: Inspira seals Rs 1,800-crore private credit for Burger King buy in India
Business.summary: Inspira Global has secured ₹1,800 crore in private credit to acquire a controlling stake in India's Burger King operator. The company has already raised ₹1,050 crore via NCDs, with the remaining ₹800 crore anticipated this month. This significant funding signals a major move into the quick-service restaurant sector.

National Affairs.title: SC: Failure to report child sex abuse invites Pocso action
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that anyone who knows about a child sex abuse case and fails to report it can face action under the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act. This decision aims to ensure that such crimes are reported and addressed promptly. The ruling emphasizes the importance of reporting child sex abuse to prevent further harm to the victims.

National Affairs.title: Now, Opposition plans to send its letter on SIR, ECI role to all Supreme Court judges
National Affairs.summary: The Opposition plans to send a letter to all Supreme Court judges regarding the role of the Special Investigation Team (SIT) and the Election Commission of India (ECI). This move comes after they did not receive a response from the Chief Justice of India. The letter is expected to highlight concerns about the SIT and ECI's actions.

National Affairs.title: Supreme Court Declares Zero Tolerance For AI-Generated Fake Legal Precedents, Directs Bar Council To Act
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has declared zero tolerance for fake or AI-generated legal precedents, which can compromise judicial integrity. The Court has directed the Bar Council of India to frame guidelines and take disciplinary action against advocates who submit fabricated legal citations. This move aims to maintain the authenticity of legal precedents and ensure the integrity of the judicial process.

National Affairs.title: ‘Only face-saving exercises’: SC raps authorities over illegal constructions in Delhi-NCR and beyond
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has criticized authorities for their inadequate response to illegal constructions in Delhi-NCR and other areas. The Court warned that it would hold officials personally responsible for such constructions and issue stringent orders against those found responsible. This decision aims to address the issue of illegal constructions and ensure that authorities take concrete actions to prevent them.

Malaysia's energy commitments with Russia and Turkmenistan are expected to bolster its energy security.

Economy.summary: The Mumbai Metropolitan Region is a significant contributor to India's economy, accounting for 6.1% of the country's GDP. Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is leaning towards a ban on cryptocurrencies, despite a large number of traders and significant holdings in India. The RBI's internal documents reveal a policy leaning toward prohibition, which could impact the country's digital asset sector. This move is driven by concerns around cryptocurrency.

Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has signaled a potential ban on cryptocurrencies, while the tax department has warned of evasion risks associated with digital assets. This development is significant for India's digital asset sector, which is awaiting clarity on future regulations and policy framework. The RBI's concerns around cryptocurrency are driving this move.

Business.summary: Nearly half of India's UDAN regional flight routes have become non-operational, despite the government launching a revamped phase with higher subsidies and airport funding. National Affairs.title: 'Only face-saving exercises': SC raps authorities over illegal constructions in Delhi-NCR and beyond
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has reprimanded authorities over illegal constructions in Delhi-NCR and other regions, warning that officials found responsible will be held personally accountable. The Court's stern stance aims to curb the menace of illegal constructions and ensure public safety. This move highlights the judiciary's efforts to address urban planning and infrastructure issues.

National Affairs.title: 'There should be no slackness': SC orders special team to survey illegal, unsafe buildings
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has ordered a special team to survey illegal and unsafe buildings in several cities, including Delhi, Gurugram, and Lucknow. The Court's directive aims to identify and address potential safety hazards and ensure that authorities take prompt action against illegal constructions. This move underscores the judiciary's concern for public safety and urban governance.

National Affairs.title: Maharashtra Sets Up 7-Member Panel to Deliberate On UCC, Draft Expected In 6 Months
National Affairs.summary: The Maharashtra government has formed a 7-member panel to draft a Uniform Civil Code (UCC), which is expected to be completed within six months. The panel, led by retired Supreme Court judge Ranjana Desai, will deliberate on the UCC and submit a draft to the state government. This development is significant, as it marks a step towards implementing a uniform civil code in the state.

National Affairs.title: Tamil Nadu Challenges Madras HC order Quashing Backward Class Reservation for Islam Converts In SC
National Affairs.summary: The Tamil Nadu government has appealed to the Supreme Court against a Madras High Court order that denied reservation rights to individuals converting to Islam. The state government's move aims to restore reservation benefits to these individuals, and the Supreme Court's decision will have implications for the state's reservation policy and social welfare schemes.

The trade embargo threat reflects the interconnectedness of global economies and politics.

Economy.summary: Experts believe that India's growth can be accelerated by increasing private investment, which can be achieved through policy reforms and coordination between the Centre and States. Deregulation and infrastructural advancements are seen as crucial elements in achieving the country's vision of a developed economy by 2047. Boosting private investment is considered essential for India's growth trajectory.

The Reserve Bank of India's liquidity infusions also contributed to favorable conditions.

Business.title: Up Ecosystem on Foreign Capital, Family Offices May Now Have to Keep it Rolling – Outlook Business
Business.summary: India's family offices are emerging as key sources of patient capital for funding start-ups, innovation, and the country's next phase of entrepreneurial growth. As foreign capital becomes less available, family offices may play a crucial role in sustaining the start-up ecosystem. This development has significant implications for India's entrepreneurial landscape.

National Affairs.title: TN in SC for quota benefits to Muslim convert
National Affairs.summary: The Tamil Nadu government has approached the Supreme Court to challenge a Madras High Court order that quashed the state's decision to allow individuals from reserved categories who convert to Islam to still receive quota benefits. This case highlights the complexities of reservation policies and their intersection with religious conversion. The outcome of this case may have implications for similar policies across the country.

National Affairs.title: Karnataka High Court notice on plea against SEC’s action
National Affairs.summary: The Karnataka High Court has issued notices to the State Election Commission, the Election Commission of India, and the State government in response to a petition challenging the SEC's decision to conduct a parallel Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls in the Mahadevapura Assembly constituency. This case raises questions about the legality of the SEC's actions and the potential impact on the electoral process. The court's decision will be closely watched for its implications on election procedures.

National Affairs.title: NCERT textbook revision
National Affairs.summary: The NCERT has revised its Class 8 Social Science textbook following a Supreme Court order, removing references to Hitler and revising the chapter on the judiciary. The revised textbook also includes changes to the section on the Congress party's stance on Partition. This revision highlights the ongoing debate about the content and accuracy of educational materials in India.

National Affairs.title: SC Says Lawyers Can't Be Blacklisted by Banks
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that banks cannot blacklist lawyers based on allegations of professional negligence, as this infringes on the jurisdiction of the Bar Councils. The court has also directed the Bar Council of India to explore the establishment of a National Legal Academy. This decision has significant implications for the legal profession and the relationship between lawyers and banks.

National Affairs.title: The fight to donate blood
National Affairs.summary: A Supreme Court challenge is underway to India's 2017 blood donor guidelines, which permanently exclude transgender persons, men who have sex with men, and female sex workers from donating blood. The challenge argues that a person's identity should not determine their eligibility to donate blood, and that the current guidelines are discriminatory. The outcome of this case may have implications for blood donation policies and the rights of marginalized communities.

The hearing highlights concerns about government secrecy and accountability. The programme's legacy continues to raise questions about the ethics of government-sponsored research and surveillance.

Geopolitics.title: 'I don't think it is possible': Keir Starmer says next PM cannot spend less time on diplomacy
Geopolitics.summary: Outgoing British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has defended the amount of time he devoted to international affairs during his tenure. He argued that the next PM cannot spend less time on diplomacy, given the complexity of global issues. Starmer's comments highlight the importance of diplomacy in modern governance.

Geopolitics.title: Keir Starmer's Legacy: Balancing Act of Diplomacy and Domestic Focus
Geopolitics.summary: Outgoing Prime Minister Keir Starmer emphasized the need for a balanced approach to international and domestic policies. He defended his foreign policy focus, citing restored global standing, despite criticism. Starmer's legacy highlights the challenges of balancing diplomatic efforts with domestic priorities in modern governance.

Economy.title: Experts Shed Light on E20 Fuel: Dispelling Myths and Highlighting Benefits
Economy.summary: Energy industry experts have clarified misconceptions about India's E20 ethanol-blended petrol mandate, highlighting its minimal impact on vehicle mileage and emphasizing its economic and environmental benefits. The policy aims to promote a cleaner and more sustainable fuel option. This development is significant for India's energy sector and its efforts to reduce carbon emissions.

Economy.title: Balancing Innovation and Stability: India's Progress on Digital Rupee Discussed
Economy.summary: The Parliamentary Standing Committee on Finance met with RBI officials to review India's Central Bank Digital Currency progress and discuss regulatory challenges, taxation issues, and policy interventions. The meeting aimed to bolster India's digital financial ecosystem. This development is crucial for India's financial sector and its adoption of digital currencies.

Economy.title: RBI Kicks Off 74th Manufacturing Sector Survey: A Key Tool for Economic Analysis
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has launched its 74th Order Books, Inventories, and Capacity Utilisation Survey for the manufacturing sector, covering April-June 2026. The survey collects operational data to aid monetary policy and captures order volumes, inventory details, and capacity use. This initiative is essential for understanding India's manufacturing sector and informing economic decisions.

Economy.title: Nomura, HSBC split on RBI rate outlook as weak monsoon fuels inflation risks
Economy.summary: Economists from Nomura and HSBC have differing outlooks on the RBI's next policy move due to a weak monsoon, which may impact India's economy, GDP growth, and food inflation. The weak monsoon has fueled inflation risks, making the RBI's decision crucial for the country's economic stability. This development is significant for India's monetary policy and its efforts to manage inflation.

Economy.title: Rupee's defence takes RBI dollar shorts to a record high
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's net short forward position in the currency market has surged to a record $106.6 billion in May, indicating significant interventions to shield the rupee from hitting new lows. The central bank faces a decision on whether to reduce its forward position or bolster reserves, which is crucial for maintaining the rupee's stability. This development is important for India's currency market and its management of foreign exchange.

Economy.title: India Inc eyes more dollar debt as RBI policy support kicks in
Economy.summary: Indian companies are increasingly opting for dollar debt due to the RBI's recent policy moves, which have lowered hedging costs and eased rules. This shift is driven by the central bank's efforts to support the economy and promote foreign investment. The development is significant for India's corporate sector and its access to foreign capital.

Business.title: India's banks are handing out more loans, but deposits aren't keeping up
Business.summary: Indian banks have seen a surge in lending, outpacing deposit growth and widening the funding gap, with the loan-to-deposit ratio reaching a decade-high. This trend is driven by demand from corporate and retail sectors, raising concerns about funding sustainability. The varied strategies of lenders have resulted in some seeing deposits decline, while others have maintained a balance.

Business.title: Operational, Says Report – Outlook Business
Business.summary: Almost half of India's UDAN regional flight routes have become non-operational due to viability gap support, infrastructure delays, and airline distress. The government has launched a revamped phase with higher subsidies and airport funding. This development is reshaping the country's flagship connectivity scheme.

Business.title: Inspira seals Rs 1,800-crore private credit for Burger King buy in India
Business.summary: Inspira Global has secured ₹1,800 crore in private credit to acquire a controlling stake in India's Burger King operator. The company has already raised ₹1,050 crore via NCDs, with the remaining ₹800 crore expected to be raised this month. This significant funding marks a major move into the quick-service restaurant sector.

Business.title: Up Ecosystem on Foreign Capital, Family Offices May Now Have to Keep it Rolling – Outlook Business
Business.summary: India's family offices are emerging as key sources of patient capital for funding start-ups, innovation, and entrepreneurial growth. They are expected to play a crucial role in sustaining the country's start-up ecosystem, which was initially built on foreign capital. Family offices are becoming increasingly important for the growth of Indian start-ups.

Business.title: India's private credit market primed for sustained growth
Business.summary: India's private credit market is poised for significant expansion, projected to reach $50 billion by 2030, driven by corporate funding needs and limitations faced by traditional banks. Real estate and infrastructure sectors are leading this growth, with promoter financing also playing a crucial role. New RBI regulations may introduce greater competition in the market.

National Affairs.title: Mumbai To Get India’s First City-Wide Master Pedestrian Network Plan, BMC Tells Supreme Court
National Affairs.summary: The BMC is preparing a city-wide master pedestrian network plan for Mumbai, aiming to create a continuous and accessible footpath network. This plan will use advanced technologies like GIS, LiDAR, and drone mapping to identify missing links and improve connectivity with public transport. The plan is expected to enhance pedestrian infrastructure in the city.

National Affairs.title: Supreme Court Sounds Alarm On Overdependence On AI For Analyses On Legal Matters
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has warned against overreliance on artificial intelligence in legal reasoning, citing instances where fake precedents generated by AI influenced court orders. The court has directed the Bar Council of India to frame guidelines and disciplinary measures to prevent misuse of AI in courts. This move aims to ensure the integrity of the legal process.

National Affairs.title: Inaccurate to say passport is only travel document, says former Supreme Court judge Madan Lokur
National Affairs.summary: Former Supreme Court judge Madan Lokur has stated that it is inaccurate to say that a passport is only a travel document. He emphasized that a person holding an Indian passport is a citizen of India, highlighting the need to clarify the legal position on this matter. This statement has implications for the understanding of citizenship and travel documents in India.

National Affairs.title: Supreme Court: After Supreme Court judgment, who will reclaim our footpaths?
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court's judgment has highlighted the need to reclaim footpaths in India, which are often occupied by vendors, parked vehicles, or other obstacles. The lack of proper pedestrian infrastructure poses a risk to citizens, particularly children and the elderly. The court's decision is expected to lead to efforts to improve footpath conditions and ensure pedestrian safety.

National Affairs.title: Shri Krishna Janmabhoomi-Shahi Idgah dispute: Hindu side attends Lok Adalat, proposes relocation of mosque
National Affairs.summary: The Hindu side in the Shri Krishna Janmabhoomi-Shahi Idgah dispute has proposed the relocation of the mosque as part of a potential amicable resolution. A special Lok Adalat was held in Mathura to explore this possibility, as directed by the Supreme Court. The proposal aims to find a peaceful solution to the long-standing dispute.

This development highlights the ongoing debate about the balance between national security and individual rights.

This development highlights the challenges of leadership in navigating complex geopolitical and domestic landscapes.

Geopolitics.title: ISI's new war on India targets PM Modi with deepfakes and disinformation
Geopolitics.summary: India's national security planners have identified a new campaign by the Inter Services Intelligence (ISI) targeting Prime Minister Narendra Modi with deepfakes and disinformation. This development highlights the evolving nature of geopolitical threats and the need for governments to address the spread of misinformation. The use of deepfakes and disinformation poses significant challenges for national security and diplomacy.

Economy.title: Balancing Innovation and Stability: India's Progress on Digital Rupee Discussed
Economy.summary: The Parliamentary Standing Committee on Finance met with RBI officials to review India's Central Bank Digital Currency progress and discuss regulatory challenges, taxation issues, and policy interventions. Economy.title: RBI Kicks Off 74th Manufacturing Sector Survey: A Key Tool for Economic Analysis
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has launched its 74th survey for the manufacturing sector, covering April-June 2026, to collect operational data for monetary policy. The survey will capture order volumes, inventory details, and capacity use, aiding economic analysis. This initiative is a key tool for understanding the manufacturing sector's performance and its impact on the economy.

Economy.title: Nomura, HSBC split on RBI rate outlook as weak monsoon fuels inflation risks
Economy.summary: Economists from Nomura and HSBC have differing views on the RBI's next policy move due to a weak monsoon, which may fuel inflation risks. They assess the impact on India's economy, including GDP growth, food inflation, and the rupee. This split in outlook reflects the uncertainty surrounding the RBI's future actions.

Economy.title: Rupee's defence takes RBI dollar shorts to a record high
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's net short forward position in the currency market has surged to a record $106.6 billion in May, indicating significant interventions to shield the rupee. The central bank faces a decision on whether to reduce its forward position or bolster reserves, as the rupee has recovered from its record low due to capital inflows and policy measures.

Economy.title: India Inc eyes more dollar debt as RBI policy support kicks in
Economy.summary: Indian firms are increasingly opting for dollar debt due to the RBI's moves to lower hedging costs and ease rules. This shift is driven by central bank policies in February and June, which have made offshore funds more attractive than rupee loans. As a result, local firms are aggressively swapping out rupee loans for external commercial borrowings.

Business.title: India's banks are handing out more loans, but deposits aren't keeping up
Business.summary: Indian banks have seen a surge in lending, outpacing deposit growth and widening the funding gap, with the loan-to-deposit ratio reaching a decade-high. This trend is driven by demand from corporate and retail sectors, raising concerns about funding sustainability. The varied strategies of lenders have resulted in some deposits declining while others have maintained a balance.

Business.title: Operational, Says Report – Outlook Business
Business.summary: Almost half of India's UDAN regional flight routes have become non-operational, despite the government launching a revamped phase with higher subsidies and airport funding. The non-operational routes are due to viability gap support, infrastructure delays, and airline distress, which are reshaping the country's flagship connectivity scheme. This development affects the country's regional air connectivity.

Business.title: Inspira seals Rs 1,800-crore private credit for Burger King buy in India
Business.summary: Inspira Global has secured ₹1,800 crore in private credit to acquire a controlling stake in India's Burger King operator. The funding is a significant move into the quick-service restaurant sector, with ₹1,050 crore already raised via NCDs and the remaining ₹800 crore expected to be raised this month. This deal highlights the growing interest in India's food business sector.

Business.title: Up Ecosystem on Foreign Capital, Family Offices May Now Have to Keep it Rolling – Outlook Business
Business.summary: India's family offices are emerging as key sources of patient capital for funding start-ups, innovation, and the country's next phase of entrepreneurial growth. This development is crucial as India's start-up ecosystem has been largely dependent on foreign capital. Family offices are now expected to play a significant role in sustaining the ecosystem.

Business.title: India's private credit market primed for sustained growth
Business.summary: India's private credit market is expected to reach $50 billion by 2030, driven by increasing corporate funding needs and limitations faced by traditional banks. The real estate and infrastructure sectors are leading this growth, with promoter financing also playing a crucial role. New RBI regulations allowing bank acquisition financing may introduce greater competition in the market.

National Affairs.title: Mumbai To Get India’s First City-Wide Master Pedestrian Network Plan, BMC Tells Supreme Court
National Affairs.summary: The BMC is creating India's first city-wide master pedestrian network plan for Mumbai, aiming to create a continuous and accessible footpath network and improve connectivity with public transport. This plan uses advanced technologies like GIS, LiDAR, and drone mapping. The Supreme Court will hear the matter next on September 3.

National Affairs.title: Supreme Court Sounds Alarm On Overdependence On AI For Analyses On Legal Matters
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has warned against overreliance on artificial intelligence in legal reasoning, citing instances where fake precedents generated by AI influenced court orders. The court has directed the Bar Council of India to frame guidelines and disciplinary measures to prevent misuse of AI in courts. This move aims to ensure the integrity of the legal process.

National Affairs.title: Inaccurate to say passport is only travel document, says former Supreme Court judge Madan Lokur
National Affairs.summary: Former Supreme Court judge Madan Lokur has stated that it is inaccurate to say a passport is only a travel document, suggesting that it is also a proof of citizenship. He emphasized the need to clarify the legal position that a person holding an Indian passport is a citizen of India. This clarification is important for understanding the rights and privileges associated with Indian citizenship.

National Affairs.title: The right to walk: After Supreme Court judgement, who will reclaim our footpaths?
National Affairs.summary: A recent Supreme Court judgment has highlighted the importance of pedestrian infrastructure, following the death of a child who was killed by a tanker while walking to school due to the absence of a footpath. The judgment emphasizes the need for reclaiming footpaths and ensuring the right to walk safely. This issue is crucial for urban planning and pedestrian safety in India.

National Affairs.title: Shri Krishna Janmabhoomi-Shahi Idgah dispute: Hindu side attends Lok Adalat, proposes relocation of mosque
National Affairs.summary: A special Lok Adalat was held in Mathura as part of the Supreme Court's efforts to resolve the Shri Krishna Janmabhoomi-Shahi Idgah dispute amicably. The Hindu side attended the Lok Adalat and proposed the relocation of the mosque. This development is part of the ongoing efforts to find a peaceful resolution to the dispute.

Geopolitics.title: How Indian Intelligence Navigated Cold War Tides in Seychelles
Geopolitics.summary: A former Indian security official recounted the 1987 Indian security mission to Seychelles, which involved navigating Cold War geopolitics and mercenary threats. The mission showcased India's growing influence in the Indian Ocean region during the Cold War era. The story provides insight into India's historical geopolitical engagements

Geopolitics.title: Diplomatic Shifts: Syria and Hezbollah's Possible Engagement
Geopolitics.summary: Syria's Foreign Minister hinted at potential engagements with Hezbollah, indicating a possible shift in the region's geopolitical dynamics. The development comes as Syria navigates complex alliances following its civil war, while being cautious not to interfere in Lebanon's affairs. The move may impact the regional balance of power

Geopolitics.title: Oil prices down but firms still processing crude bought amid war, says Minister Puri
Geopolitics.summary: Union Oil Minister Hardeep Puri stated that despite decreased international oil prices, oil marketing companies are still processing crude oil purchased during the height of the war in West Asia. This situation has resulted in significant losses for the companies. The development highlights the impact of global conflicts on India's energy sector

Economy.title: Balancing Innovation and Stability: India's Progress on Digital Rupee Discussed
Economy.summary: The Parliamentary Standing Committee on Finance met with RBI officials to review India's Central Bank Digital Currency progress, focusing on regulatory challenges and taxation issues. This discussion is crucial for India's digital financial ecosystem. The meeting aimed to bolster the country's digital asset landscape.

Economy.title: RBI Kicks Off 74th Manufacturing Sector Survey: A Key Tool for Economic Analysis
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India launched its 74th survey for the manufacturing sector, covering April-June 2026, to collect operational data for monetary policy. The survey captures order volumes, inventory details, and capacity use, and is a key tool for economic analysis. This initiative will aid in understanding the manufacturing sector's performance.

Economy.title: Nomura, HSBC split on RBI rate outlook as weak monsoon fuels inflation risks
Economy.summary: Economists from Nomura and HSBC have differing views on the RBI's next policy move due to a weak monsoon, which may impact India's economy, GDP growth, and food inflation. The weak monsoon poses inflation risks and affects the rupee and balance of payments. The differing views highlight the uncertainty surrounding the RBI's next move.

Economy.title: Rupee's defence takes RBI dollar shorts to a record high
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's net short forward position in the currency market reached a record $106.6 billion in May, indicating significant interventions to shield the rupee. The central bank faces a decision on whether to reduce its forward position or bolster reserves, as the rupee has recovered from its record low. This move aims to stabilize the currency.

Economy.title: India Inc eyes more dollar debt as RBI policy support kicks in
Economy.summary: Indian firms are opting for dollar debt over rupee loans due to the RBI's moves to lower hedging costs and ease rules. This shift is driven by central bank policies in February and June, making offshore funds more attractive. Local firms are aggressively swapping out rupee loans for offshore funds.

Economy.title: Easing oil prices, supply chains to soften external pressures on India: Finance ministry
Economy.summary: The finance ministry believes that easing oil prices and improving supply chains will soften external pressures on India's economy, despite global uncertainties. However, monsoon vagaries and geopolitical shifts pose risks to growth and inflation. The ministry emphasizes the need for proactive policy measures, such as building national buffer stocks and reorienting farm policies.

Business.title: India's private credit market primed for sustained growth
Business.summary: India's private credit market is expected to reach $50 billion by 2030, driven by increasing corporate funding needs and limitations faced by traditional banks. Real estate and infrastructure sectors are leading this growth, with promoter financing also playing a crucial role. New RBI regulations may introduce greater competition to this alternative capital source.

Business.title: PlayBlue raises $2.7 million in seed funding to expand omnichannel sports retail business
Business.summary: Sports retail startup PlayBlue has secured $2.7 million in seed funding to launch its omnichannel platform and expand its business. The company aims to capture India's growing sports participation by offering a wide range of brands. PlayBlue plans to expand significantly, targeting substantial revenue and a large user community within five years.

Business.title: Arvog Raises Rs 50 Crore Through NCD Issuance from Franklin Templeton to Accelerate Growth and Business Expansion

The court's intervention will prevent the withdrawal of cases until further review.

This ruling highlights the importance of transparency and honesty in electoral processes. The court's decision will have implications for candidates and political parties in future elections.

National Affairs.title: SC nixes rulings by NCLT, NCLAT based on fake AI citations
